name: monitor
context: Inspect Azure resources with metrics, activity logs, and Log Analytics queries before and after operational changes
info: Azure Monitor is the cross-cutting observability layer for Azure resources. Agents use it to answer the first operational questions after a deploy or incident: did the control plane report a failure, is the resource emitting unhealthy metrics, and what do workspace logs say over the relevant time window? The key CLI surfaces are `az monitor metrics list`, `az monitor activity-log list`, and `az monitor log-analytics query`.
triggers: find why an azure deployment failed
debug an app service incident in azure
look up azure control plane events
run a kusto query against azure logs
body:
# Azure Monitor

Azure Monitor answers three different questions, and agents should pick the right query surface for each one:

- Metrics: is the resource healthy over time?
- Activity log: what management-plane action happened, by whom, and when?
- Log Analytics: what do the workload or platform logs say in Kusto?

~~~bash
# Metrics on a specific resource ID
az monitor metrics list \
  --resource "$RESOURCE_ID" \
  --metric "Percentage CPU" \
  --interval PT1M

# Activity log for the past hour in a resource group
az monitor activity-log list -g rg-dojo --offset 1h --max-events 50

# Kusto query against a Log Analytics workspace
az monitor log-analytics query \
  --workspace "$LOG_ANALYTICS_WORKSPACE_ID" \
  --analytics-query "AzureActivity | summarize count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)" \
  --timespan P1D
~~~

Metrics are best for trend and saturation signals. Activity log is best for create, update, delete, RBAC, and policy failures. Log Analytics is the deepest surface once data is already flowing into a workspace.

Use this node immediately after deployments from [[azure/app-service]], [[azure/functions]], or [[azure/aks]], and pair it with application-layer telemetry like [[sentry]] when platform health and application exceptions need to be correlated.

sections.title: Metrics queries
sections.body: `az monitor metrics list --resource ... --metric ...` queries time-series data for a specific resource. Use it for CPU, latency, transactions, saturation, and other numeric health signals. Tune the `--interval`, `--aggregation`, and `--filter` options when the default series is too coarse or too broad.

sections.title: Activity log queries
sections.body: `az monitor activity-log list` is the management-plane history: who deleted a resource, whether a deployment failed, when policy blocked an operation, or what RBAC change preceded an outage. Filter by resource group, resource ID, correlation ID, or time range. This is the first stop when Azure itself accepted or rejected an operation.

sections.title: Log Analytics and Kusto
sections.body: `az monitor log-analytics query --workspace ... --analytics-query ...` runs Kusto over workspace data. The workspace argument is the workspace GUID, not the display name. Use it when platform data, application logs, or diagnostic tables are already routed to Log Analytics and you need structured investigation over a specific timespan.

links.context: Check web app health and deployment fallout after App Service changes
Inspect serverless deployment and runtime issues after Function App changes
Correlate AKS control-plane or cluster rollout issues with metrics and events
Pair Azure platform signals with application exceptions and traces
Continue deeper into workload debugging after AKS-level health checks

related.note: Sentry complements Azure Monitor with application-level errors and traces
Platform monitoring is part of every App Service deployment loop
Function App operations should be followed by metric or log inspection

author: dojo-community
